mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) such as Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5, and others. It provides a simple interface for deploying and interacting with these models in a Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include ease of use versus the complexity of managing underlying infrastructure.

steps.title: Install the Ollama CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Ollama platform.

```bash

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

irm https://ollama.com/install.ps1 | iex

```

> Use this command for Linux and macOS.
> Use this command for Windows.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create ollama-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Ollama on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys the Ollama application on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add ollama-helm https://otwld.github.io/ollama-helm/

helm install ollama ollama-helm/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama --create-namespace

```

> Add the Ollama Helm repository.
> Deploy Ollama with the specified version.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures necessary components like autoscaling and monitoring for your Ollama deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://ollama.com/config/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://ollama.com/config/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Ollama deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

gcloud container clusters delete ollama-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will remove the Ollama deployment.
> This will delete the entire cluster and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade your Ollama deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade ollama ollama-helm/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade Ollama to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama Docker image may not be available or accessible.

**Fix:**
Check your Docker registry settings and ensure the image exists: 'docker pull ollama/ollama:0.17.7'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes' returns 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more nodes in the cluster are not in a ready state.

**Fix:**
Check the node status and logs: 'kubectl describe node <node-name>'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama Helm release may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Helm chart: 'helm install ollama ollama-helm/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama --create-namespace'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused' when accessing the API
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama service may not be running or exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the service status: 'kubectl get svc -n ollama' and ensure it's exposed.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models including Kimi-K2.5 and GLM-5, improved API response times.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n ollama' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
